paragraphs.text: Flix Response to the Digital Fairness Act consultation

evidence_quotes.quote: This submission focuses on the section addressing practices related to pricing.

paragraphs.text: Flix welcomes the opportunity to contribute to the European Commission's public consultation on the upcoming Digital Fairness Act. This submission focuses on the section addressing practices related to pricing. As a leading pan-European mobility provider, offering sustainable long-distance coaches and train travel across 40+ countries, Flix shares the Commission's commitment to strengthening consumer protection and ensuring that digital markets are transparent, competitive, and fair.

evidence_quotes.quote: Pricing transparency is central to consumer trust.

paragraphs.text: Pricing transparency is central to consumer trust. At the same time, it is important that measures designed to achieve it remain proportionate, evidence-based, and consistent across the EU. Flix supports initiatives that empower consumers and create legal certainty, such as clearer guidance, stronger enforcement of existing rules, and initiatives that help consumers better understand and exercise their rights when purchasing online and at the same time promote competitiveness.

evidence_quotes.quote: pricing through guidance and more consistent enforcement, such as for instance the following points.

paragraphs.text: Long-standing laws such as the Consumer Rights Directive (CRD), the Price Indications Directive (PID), the Unfair Contract Terms Directive (UCTD) and the Unfair Commercial Practices Directive (UCPD) -- all recently amended by the Enforcement and Modernisation Directive together with substantial sectoral legislation form extensive consumer acquis at EU level. At times, they are further gold-plated at the Member States level, creating a highly layered compliance landscape for both businesses and consumers to navigate. We believe that the Commission could address some of the identified aspects in the Fitness Check related to pricing through guidance and more consistent enforcement, such as for instance the following points.

evidence_quotes.quote: Our experience across Member States shows that interpretations of existing transparency obligations vary when the consumer needs to be informed about the service fee.
information to consumers upfront about the service fee (if any) before consumers start the purchasing/booking process.

paragraphs.text: Our experience across Member States shows that interpretations of existing transparency obligations vary when the consumer needs to be informed about the service fee. Therefore, we believe that establishing EU-wide guidance on fees such as when service fees should be presented to consumers could help ensure harmonized approach at the EU level. For example, it could be information to consumers upfront about the service fee (if any) before consumers start the purchasing/booking process.

evidence_quotes.quote: Dynamic pricing is a legitimate, well-established and consumer-oriented practice across several sectors, including transport.

paragraphs.text: Dynamic pricing is a legitimate, well-established and consumer-oriented practice across several sectors, including transport. By adjusting prices according to demand, timing, and availability, it enables operators to manage capacity more efficiently while offering lower fares to consumers who book early or travel during off-peak periods. This approach enhances affordability and accessibility for price-sensitive travelers and contributes to sustainability by reducing unused capacity and improving overall resource efficiency.

evidence_quotes.quote: “From” prices also play an important role in making transport services accessible.
the lowest fares are accessible to passengers who purchase in advance and/or travel during off-peak periods.

paragraphs.text: “From” prices also play an important role in making transport services accessible. By clearly indicating the lowest available fare, they show that affordable options exist. This approach helps to make travel accessible to price sensitive passengers who might not travel otherwise without requiring everyone to pay the same flat fare. It also enables operators to manage demand more efficiently, spreading journeys more evenly throughout the day or week as usually the lowest fares are accessible to passengers who purchase in advance and/or travel during off-peak periods.

evidence_quotes.quote: prices vary with timing, demand, and availability
advertised “from” prices correspond to genuine offers available to a meaningful number of passengers.

paragraphs.text: Flix considers transparency the essential element of “from” pricing. Consumers should clearly understand that prices vary with timing, demand, and availability, and that advertised “from” prices correspond to genuine offers available to a meaningful number of passengers.

Flix remains fully committed to working constructively with the Commission to ensure effective legislation that strengthens consumer trust and promotes competitiveness and remains open to provide additional evidence if needed on the impact for the transportation sector.

Flix intends to transform the collective transport sector by offering sustainable and affordable long-distance bus and train travel solutions in more than 40 countries across four continents through its brands FlixBus, FlixTrain, Kamil Koç, and Greyhound. With its asset-light business model, Flix, launched in 2013, swiftly established a market-leading position for long-distance bus travel in Europe, North America and Türkiye and is rapidly expanding further into South America and India.

Driven by increased awareness for sustainable travel, Flix aims to achieve Net Zero operations in Europe by 2040 and globally by 2050. To assess its progress within a scientifically recognized framework, Flix established near-term targets for emissions reduction with the Science Based Targets initiative.

While Flix manages the commercial side of the business such as network planning, pricing, operations control, marketing and sales, quality management and continuous product development with a data-driven approach, trusted Flix partners conduct the daily operations. The innovative combination of Flix's technology and sales platform with traditional passenger travel has turned a European start-up into a leading and globally expanding travel tech company.

On top of being sustainable, long-distance buses have an important social role. Incorporating the mode in the way forward will secure the freedom of mobility for millions of Europeans in remote & rural regions and even lesser connected urban areas that depend on long-distance buses as their often only mobility option.
The contribution of the Central European Lawyers Initiative (CELI) is provided in the attached document.
